NmDCc

5.2. All safety measures such as provision of safety helmets, belts, shoes to the workmen and
supervisory stuff employed by the contractor shall be statutory which shall be provided by the
contractor to his workmen and labour at his cost.

6, Excavation and Trenching: All trenches, 1.5 meter or more in depth, shall at all times, be supplied
with at least one ladder for each 20 meters in length or fraction thereof. Ladder shall be extended
from bottom of trench to at least meter above surface of the ground, Sides of trench which is 1.5
meters or more in depth shall be stepped back to give suitable slope or securely held by timber
bracing, so as to avoid the danger of sides collapsing. Fxcavated material shall not be placed within
1,5 meters of edge of trench or half of depth of trench, whichever is more, Cutting shall be done

from top to bottom, Under no circumstances shall undermining or undercutting be done.

7, Demolition: Before any demolition work is commenced and also during the process of the work:

3) All rvads and open areas adjacent to the work site shall either be closed or suitably protected.

b) = No electric: cable or apparatus which is liable to be a source of danger over a cable or apparatus
used by operator shall remain electrically charged.

¢) All practical steps shall be taken to prevent danger to persons employed from risk or ire or
explosion, or flooding, No floor, roof, or other part of a building shall be so overloaded with debris
or materials as to render it unsafe.

8, Allnecessary personal safety equipment as considered adequate by the Engineer-in-Charge shall
he available for use of persons cmployed on the site and mentioned in a cundition suitable for
immediate use; and the contractor shall take adequate steps to ensure proper use of equipment
by those concerned,

a) Workers employed on mixing asphaltic materials cement and lime mortars/ concrete shall be
provided with protective footwear and protective goggles.

b) ‘Those engaged in handling any material, which is injurious to cyes, shall be provided with
protective goggles,

c) Those engayed in welding works shall be provided with welder's protective eye-shields, plives
and foutwears,

d) Stone breakers shall be provided with protective goggles and protective clothing and seated at
sufficiently safe intervals.

2) When workers are employed in sewers and manholes, which are in use the contractor shall ensure

that manhole covers are opened and manholes are ventilated at least for an hour before workers
are allowed to getinto them, Manholes so opened shall be cordoned off with suitable railing and

provided with warning signals or boards to prevent accident to public.

f) The Contractor shall not employ men helow the age of 18 years and women on the work of
painting with products containing lead in any form. Whenever men above the age of 18 years are
employed on the work of lead painting, the following precautions shall be taken:

i) No paint containing lead or lead products shall be used except in the form of paste or ready-made
paint.

ii) Suitable facemasks shall be supplied for use by workers when paint is applied in the form of spray
or surface having lead paint dry rubbed and scrapped.

iii) Overalls shall be supplied by the contractor to workmen and adequate facilities shall be provided
to enable working painters to wash during and on cessation of work.

| 9, When work is done near any place where there is risk of drowning, all necessary equipment shall

be provided and kept ready for use and all necessary steps talcen for promptrescuc of any person
in danger und adequate provision made for prompt first aid treatment of all injuries likely to be
sustained during the course of the work.

10, Use of hoisting machines and tackles including their attachments, anchorage and supports shall
conform to the following:

i) ‘These shall be of good mechanical construction, sound material and adequate strength and free
from patent defects and shall be kept in good working order and properly maintained.

ii) Every rope used in hoisting or lowering or materials or as a means of suspension shall he of
durable quality and adequate strength and [ree from patent defects.

14. Every crane driver or hoisting appliance operator shall be properly qualified and no person under
age of 21 years shall be in charge of any hoisting machine including any scaffold or pive signal to
operator,

12. In case of every hoisting machine and of every chain rink hook, shackle swivel and pulley block
used in hoisting or lowering or as means of suspension, safe working load shall be ascertained by
adequate means, Every hoisting machine and all gear referred to above shall be plainly marked.

A First ~ Aid box shall be kept by the contractor at the site office lo meet any emergency that may arise.

INTER PLANT STANDARD POR SAFETY IN INDUSTRY
AFETY IN CONTRACT WORKS 'SS:1-11-011-01

No corresponding Indian Standard exists

FOREWORD

This Inter Plant Standard fortnulated by the Standard Committee on Personnel Safety Appliances &

Procedures, IPSS 1:11 with the active participation of the representatives of plants and associated

organizations in the field, was adopted in May, 2001.

This is one of the series of Inter Plant Standards in the area of the safety in the plants with a view to

providing guidance to all concerned in accident prevention.

1.0 SCOPE OF WORK

id Safety is the responsibility of the Contractor and his staff / employees/ workmen engaged/
deployed for execution of work under the Contract, individually and collectively, For this purpose,
the Contractor staff means and includes all his associates and sub-contractors / vendors/ sub-
vendors and their stafl/ employees/ workmen deployed for execution of the work covered under
the contract. The Contractor shall ensure that his workmen participate in the safety awareness,
health care and safety training programmes whenever such programmes are organised by the
employer or the Contractor,

12 The Contractor's scope of work shall include, but not limited to execution uf work/ contract,
adequate safety arrangements for men machinery ete. engaged during the execution of the
Contract.

13 While executing the Contract, the Contractor / his supervisor has to ensure safety of the
surroundings with regard to employer's work place/ site and other contractor's men/ machine/
materials/ system etc.

2.0 DETAILED PROCEDURE/ PROTOCOL

2.1 Before starting the work, a safe work procedure/ protocol shall be prepared and signed jointly by
the executing department, representative of Safety Deptt. and the Contractor or his representative,
This procedure/ protocol shall he prepared by breaking the whole job into small clements and
listing them separately in the sequence, Against these elements, the agency responsible for doing

GENERAL TERMS & CONDITIONS

1,0 SUPERVISOR

11 ‘The Contractor shall engape qualified and competent supervisors for executing the
Contract, The supervisor must be able to i) read drawing ; ii) understand the safety aspect
of the job ; iii) take necessary precautionary measures ; iv) ensure leadership for the sate
execution of the job.

2.0 SAFETY SUPERVISION

21 The Contractor shall provide at least one competent full-time safely supervisor who shall
be nominated for each contract depending on the number of persons employed and safety
requirements of the job including round the clock supervision on safety aspects,
wherever required.

3.0 GATE PASS

3.1 The Contractor shall, in accordance with prescribed procedure, obtain gate passes (photo
passes) from Employer's Security Department who shall make it available to the
Contractor, to enable the Contractor and his staff / employees/ workmen to enter the work
area inside the plant as well as un-enclosed (open) work area like township, ‘The
Contractor shall make arrangement for instant photography for those contract workers
who may be required to go to work in emergency and do not have photo passes,

40 SAFETY INDUCTION

41 In order to provide basic industrial safety training to the workers of Contractor,
Employer will provide on request, the general safety induction to the workmen/
supervisor at Safety Engineering Department. The Contractor shall submit the list of
persons with full details for the job of safety induction to the Engineer for arranging the
same, The Contractor shall not engage untrained personnel for executing any Contract
inside the works. Job safety induction is a compulsory thing for every job to start with,

4.2 If at any point of time, Contractor feels or needs any special safety training for
carrying out a particular job the same shall be arranped by the concerned Engineer of the
Employer, Por this, the Contractor shall inform the concerned Engincer of the Employer
well in advance of commencement of such job.

4,3 The Contractor shall motivate and encourage his employees/ workmen to make
personnel contribution towards enhancing safety on the basis of their knowledge and
experience.

representative of the Contractor,

while executing the job/ Contract during the particular day.
AT Contractors shall follow all special safety precautions issued by the executing authority or
his site engineer from Lime to time,
5.0 LEGAL AND PENAL
5.1 The Contractor shall abide by the provisions of all statutory acts and rules as
applicable and maintain register/ documents, submit periodic reports and returns
furnish information/ report relating lo accidents occurring in the area of work to
competent authority in time.
5.2 The Contractor shall be liable for prosecution and penalty in accordance with as
prescribed under the law of land, terms & conditions of Contract as well as rules of the
Company relating to safety, for not observing those provisions hy the Contractor or his
employees/ workmen.

5.3 The Contractor shall keep the Company indemnified apainst all losses/ claims due to
accidents/ injuries/ damages caused at the Contractor's works site, He shall be fully
responsible for accidents caused due to the Contractor or his agents or workmen's
negligence or carelessness in regard to observance of the safety requirements and shall be
liable to pay compensation for injuries.

5.4 The Lingineer or Head of the Safety Department or their authorised hominees, upon their
satisfaction that the Contractor is nat conforming to the safety requirements, may direct
stoppage of work and direct the Contractor to remedy the defects or supply the facility/
equipment as the case may he, The Contractor shall not proceed with the work until he
has complied with such directions to the satisfaction of the Engineer/ Safety
Department,

5.5 \fa Driver or any staff of the Contractor is caupht in theft case or in any unauthorised
mavement of materials or in the activity which is punishable under the law or not
authorised by the plant, the Contractor shall bear the full responsibility for the foss and
other consequences which may result Lo the plant due to such illegal / unauthorised acts
besides the action to terminate the Contract by the plant.

5.6 The Contractor shall he responsible to ensure that vehicles belonging to him are notdriven

in reckless or rash manner so as to become a potential threat to the safety of the traffic in
the plant or township area. The drivers of the vehicles shal) he made to adhered to the
speed limits wherever applicable. Failure to comply with the above, may result in
termination of the Contract.
In case of accident or injury or damage caused by the Contractor's vehicle or staff to any
person or property, the financial responsibility to compensate be borne solely hy the
Contractor and this amount may, at the discretion of the Competent Authority of the Plant,
be recovered from the bills or security or other deposits of the Contractor,

5.7 The Contractor shall ensure that all his employees/ workmen are covered under
“Workmen Compensation Act” and shall pay compensation to his workmen as and when
the eventuality for the same arises.

5.8 To ensure effective enforcement of the rules and regulations relating to safety
precautions, the arrangements made by the Contractor shall be open to inspection by
the Engineer or his representatives, Safety Engineer and Labour Officer.

39 The Contractor shall enclose along with his first bill a true copy of the Safety
Induction Farm (Annexure-1A) duly certified hy the Executing Authority reparding use
of safety appliances, following of safety instructions and observance of all safety laws,
failing which the bill will not be processed for payment,

5.10 Notwithstanding the above clauses, there is nothing in those to exempt the Contractor
from the operation of any other Act or rule in Republic of India for the safety of men
and materials.

5.11 The safety violations have been classified in to five categories (I to V) as enlisted at
Annexure-1, Without prejudice to the right conferred by the Clause 5.4 above for
stoppage of work for violation of safety requirement, the contractor shall be liable for
penalty at the rates indicated below depending upon the category of violation.

Category Penalty
Rs./Violation
I, 1000/-
Il. 2500/-
Ill, 7500/-
IV. 20,000/-
V. 3 lakhs or 20% of the contract value whichever is lower,
In the event of repetition of violation for third time and onwards by the contractors, the
penalty shall be imposed at double of the penalty rate for the second violation.
6.0 PPE & SAFETY APPLIANCES
6.1 The Gontractor shall provide all the PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) and safety
uppliances required to carry out the job to all the workmen deployed by the Contractor
and also ensure that his workmen use those PPE and safety appliances while on the job.
The Contractor shall not pay any cash amountin lieu of PPE to the workers.

6.2 The PPE and safety appliances provided by the Contractor shall be of the standard as
prescribed by the Inter Plant Standardization in Industry (IPSS) or by Bureau of
Indian Standards (BIS). If materials conforming to IPSS or BIS standards are notayailable,
PPE and safety appliances shall be procured by the Contractor as approved by the
Employer.

6.3 If the Contractor fuils to ensure provision of safety appliances required to carry out the
job safely to the workmen and that his workmen do not use the PPE and safety appliances
as needed for safe working, Engincer and the Safety Engineering Department upon the
satistaction that the Contractoris not conforming to the safety requirement may direct the
Contractor for stoppage of work and require the Contractor to remedy the defects.

7.0 SITE CLEARANCE

71 Prior to commencement of work, the Contractor shall obtain a clearance from the Safety
Engineering Department of the Employer and concerned department/ section of the
Employer where the job is to be carried out. The Employer/ Engincer shall not permit the
Contractor to commence the work unless Contractor or his representative produces such
clearance certificate, The Department concerned shall grant such clearance after
ensuring that:

a) ‘The Contractor himself has undergone Safety Induction Training organised by the
Employer's Safety Engineering Department in the preceding twelve months,

b) The Contractor's workers, identified for particular work / Department, have
undergone Safety Induction Training in the preceding twelve months.

c) The Contractor has arranged PPR and Safety appliances for himself and for all his
workmen, 2s required.

d) ‘The Contractor possesses work permit issued by the Employer or Employer's
representative for which the “Work Clearance Form "(Atnexure-1B) shall be filled
it triplicate by the Contractor for record of:

i) Safety
li) Department/ Section of the Employer granting Clearance and
iii) Contractor

ce) The Contractor and his supervisors along with the Contract documents and job
instructions received from the employer, will come to Employer’s Safety
Engineering Department where the Contractor will obtain Safety Rule Book tor
Contractor and seek clarifications, if any, The Contractor is not permitted to stop
the job without clearance from Employer's Safety Lngineering Department.

fy The Contractor shall possess handling equipment, tools & tackles of adequate
capacity and tested quality.

height by Contractor's Workers” from the Engineer of the company or the
companies Safety Department before commencing the following:

a) Working in an operating plant / equipment; |

91 The Contractor shall ensure that his employees/ workmen subject themselves to such

a record of the same.

9.2 The Contractor shall not permit any emplayee/ workmen to enter the work area
under the influence of alcohol.

113  Electrician/ Other Electrical Personnel
The Contractor shall engage qualified and competent electricians and other
electrical personnel while working on electrical lines (which may be High Tension,
Medium Tension and Low ‘lension electrical lines) for safe execution of Contract. The
electricians and other electrical personnel must possess requisite certificate issued from
competent authority.
Electrical Equipment
a) The Contractor shall ensure proper earthing of all electrical equipment/ machinery
prior to start of the machine. ‘The Contractor shall also ensure daily examination of
the earthing of equipment/ machinery prior to start of the job in order to avoid
electrical hazards,
b) The Contractor shal! ensure use of proper cable (may be welding cable or power
cable or control cable). The jomts of the cables shall be perfectly insulated. The
Contractor shall also ensure the use of proper plugs, sockets and other electrical
fittings while executing any Contract, ELCB of appropriate rating shall be used by
the Contractor in the power distribution system for safety of personnel working.
114 Scaffolding/ lifting/lowering / dismantling of equipment or fabricated structural
or any other materials.

i) For safe execution of the Contract, Contractor shall make a programme of a piece/
whole job, in sequence of steps involved in execution the required safety
measures to be taken during the execution of the job.

ii) The Contractor shall ensure the use of sufficiently strong scaffolding with
sufficiently strong hand railings during the execution of the job. 'The Employer will
provide prints of "Standard Scaffolding Drawing” on request by the Contractor.
Seaffold/ platform to be used by more than 2 persons has to be approved by the
Site Engineer and the concerned Safety Organisation, jointly. The Contractor shall
censure the use of scaffoldings at construction site hy his personnel working at

height, fabricated as per standard drawing for scaffolding. If the scaffolding as per
the standard drawing is not found suitable at site, the Contractor shall make
necessary modification to strengthen the same. For any civil engineering, job, the
Contractor will provide adequate support before casting ur any superstructure
and provide approach, scaffolding ete. in such a manner thatis safe, easy for
movement of men, women and materials.
iii) The Contractor shall make his own arrangement for the area lighting/ spot

115 House keeping

i) The Contractor shall ensure pood housekeeping in his working area, fabricating
yard as well as at erection site, The safe approach to the working place must be
ensured, Plice for keeping raw materials, scmi-finished materials should be
clearly marked with proper identification. Machine component shall be kept
properly under shed,

ii) The Contractor shall keep the scrap material at one place duly barricated and
shall remove the same regularly from the work place.

iii) The Contractor shall stack properly the fabricated or any other items likely to be
erected very shortly at erection site,in such a way that the materials do not cause
congestion at site or create unsafe condition for free and sate approach.

iv) The Contractor should put up a board (minimum 4! x 3°) indicating the name of
the Contractor, details ofthe job, order No., starting date and likely completion
date of the job. On completion of the job, the Contractor shall clear the site (yood
materials and scrap), The board so displayed shall be taken out of the site. Any
material left after the job is over will be picked up by the Employer and the
Contractor will be charged back for the same.

v) Barrication
The Contractor shall ensure barrication of the unsafe area, which can lead to
accidents to any unknown person working in thal area, In order to remove unsafe
condition and prevent accidents, this unsafe area such as trench/ pit/ foundation,
any type of opening made or left in any floor of the building including staircase
etc, must be burricated.

The hand railings shall be immediately provided in all the floors, stairs cte.
before proceeding for erection/ dismantling,

persons working on the ground level on the periphery of the pit which is 10 meter or

height and issue height pass. Height pass issued will be in the name of individual
worker and its validity will be for a maximum period of 01 year only.

c) On expiry, certificates of safety training for working at height as per 4(a) and height
passes as per 4(b) will be renewed after the workers passes the tests and imparted
training once again

monitored as per the Operational Control Procedures and laid down criteria and
records shall be maintained.

hs When aninjury occurs to «Contractor's employee while inside the factory, the injured
person should be sent to Plant Medical Unit immediately, with this form in triplicate.
ae The Medical Officer on duty at Plant Medical Unit will retain one copy and send one copy
to Safety Engincering Department. The third copy shall be returned to the Contractor
concerned after duly filling up the forms.
In case, the Contractor takes the injured person ta his private doctor, he shall do so by
giving a written undertaking to the doctor in Plant Medital Unitand to the Manaper, Safety
Engincering Department.
If the private doctor declares the injured person unfit for duty more than 2 days, this
becomes a reportable accident under the Factories Act and the Contractor shall
immediately report this to the Manager, Safety Engineering, Department for sending
reports to concerned Government agencies.
The Contractor shall also keep the manager (Safety) infortn about :
a) The condition of the injured person;
b) The period of disability and
c) Any loss of earning capacity certified by the doctor,
The Contractor shall produce the fitness certificate from the attending doctor after the
injured person is declared fit for duty.

xxiii, The successful bidder will follow the holiday calendar of NMDC LIMITED. They have to work
late after working hours and attend issues on weekends/holidays as and when required
without any additional cost to NMDC LIMITED,

xxiv. Successful bidder shall keep each & every record, logs of the proceedings of each phases of the
project in writing to avoid any debatable issues of delaying the project on accounts of reasons
attributable to both the sides

xxv. Successful bidder shall submit monthly detailed report to GM(Mining)-ERP NMDC Limited on
Helpdesk ticket analysis with all status, Change Request status and status of all service
requests logged with OEM SAP and also make suitable presentation to the management on the
above issues as and when required. Additionally, successful bidder may share their comments
on quality of tickets, any major issues encountered & resolved, transactional difficulties and
adoption of best practices to help in reducing the tickets volume further.

xxvi. All the equipments, systems, and structures of the SAP ERP System will be maintained in the
form and condition as handed over to it by the Existing Contractor during the Takeover Phase
and it will provide services in relation to all these equipments, systems, and structures of the
SAP ERP System as per the said condition during the period of contract without any additional
cost to the NMDC and requesting any document from the NMDC evidencing performance
parameters of the SAP ERP System at the time of handover from the Existing Contractor.

xxvii, The scope of Services shall also include all such services which may not have heen specifically
mentioned in this Tender, or the Technical Specifications, or the Functional Specifications hut
which may be necessary for the successful fulfilment of Contractor's obligation mentioned in
this Tender as per Prudent Operating and Maintenance Practices and such services shall be
performed by the successful bidder without any additional cost to the NMDC,

Role of SAP Functional Consultant

transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System.

& User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an abstract and algorithmic

is not available, solution may be provided through configuration and customizing the respective
business area and ensure to map) the system to achieve the business requirements

* Provide the Documentation for the settings and Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) that allow
other consultants to do further changes or modifications if required.

« Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever required.

ANNEXURE - B
Scope of Work for the proposal of Selection of Service Provider for Annual
Maintenance Contract for SAP S/4 HANA at NMDC
The scope of work in this tender includes
> ‘lo provide all the services (onsite support) required for successful and uninterrupted
day to day operations/transactions, maintenance, update and upgrade of the SAP ERP
System at all locations for the following modules of SAP S/4 HANA and entire SAP
landscape implemented in NMDC Limited (NMDC) including developments to be made
as per the requirements of business notified from time to time.

a) FINANCE & CONTROLLING (FICO)

b) MATERIALS MANAGEMENT (MM) INCLUDING SUPPLIER RELATIONSHIP
MANAGEMENT (SRM)

c) PRODUCTION PLANNING (PP)

d) PLANT MAINTENACE (PM)

e) HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT (HCM) / EMPLOYEE SELF SERVICE (ESS) /
MANAGEMENT SELF SERVICE (MSS)

f} PROJECT SYSTEM (PS)

£) SALES & DISTRIBUTION (SD)

h) QUALITY MANAGEMENT (QM)

i) ENVIRONMENT, HEALTH & SAFETY (EHS)

j) ADVANCED BUSINESS APPLICATION PROGRAMMING (ABAP)

k) BUSINESS APPLICATION SYSTEMS INTEGRATED SOLUTION (BASIS)

1) MANUFACTURING INTEGRATION INTELLIGENCE (MII)

m) FILE LIFECYCLE MANAGEMNET (FLM), IORI APPS, MANAGEMENT
DASHBOARDS, NON-SAP PORTALS (PYTHON BASED PORTAL LIKE MDM, E-
MEASUREMENT BOOK ETC DEVELOPED IN-HOUSE)

In respect of NMDC, support shall be extended from Head Office, Hyderabad to all
locations. The major locations of NMDC are mentioned hereunder for information:

i. BIOM, Kirandul Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

ii. BIOM, Bacheli Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

iii, DIOM, Donimalai Complex, Bellary (Karnataka)

iv. Pellet Plant, Donimalai, Bellary (Karnataka)

v. DMP, Panna. (Madhya Pradesh)

vi. SIU, Paloncha (Andhra Pradesh)

vii. Slurry Pipe Line (SPL), Jagdalpur, (Chhattisgarh)
viii. R&D Centre, Ilyderahad (‘l'elangana)
ix. Coal Division, Ranchi
x. Regional Offices Delhi, Raipur, Visakhapatnam, Chennai, Bengaluru, Mumbai,
Bhubaneshwar, Kolkata

SCOPE OF WORK for SAP Support Service
The broad scope of the Functional and Technical consultant for SAP Support
Services including enhancement / customization of the SAP application in NMDC
mentioned hereunder. lt may note that the scope mentioned hereunder is not
exhaustive:
SPECIFIC REQUIREMENT:

i. The successful bidder is required to provide technical & functional support
services which include the identification and rectification of all defects/bugs.

ii. The successful bidder is required to assist in providing solution for recovery
from transaction errors, system errors, hardware failures and/or other
application, modification, enhancement issues ete,

iii. The successful hidder is required to assist in performing period end closing
activities (Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually) including Tax reporting,
Regulatory Compliance etc.

iv. The support team should be based at NMDC Head Office, Hyderabad on full time
basis in respect of NMDC to facilitate timely resolution of issues.

v. Enhancements/developments, patches, version upgrades, service packs etc. (as
applicable) are to be installed time to time where ever required on release by
OEM in consultation with the company including third party solutions
implemented and integrated with SAP. Each upgrade should be accompanied by
a detailed document containing the step-by-step procedure for effecting the
changes to the system by ensuing an established change management process.

vi. The successful bidder shall review and adopt the Governance, Risk & Compliance
(GRC) in the system to streamline and have better control across the transactions
of the company.

vii, ‘The Successful Bidder shall assign the Roles and Authorizations to end users as
and when required as per approval without delay to ensure no business
transactions was halted for want of Roles & Authorizations.

viii, The successful bidder shall provide contact numbers, email addresses, escalation
matrix ofa dedicated support team. NMDC shall be kept informed well in advance
in case any changes are being madc in the contact details. -——~

ix. ‘The successful bidder shall submit detailed documentation (e.g. ‘echnical
documents, Configuration documents, Functional document if any) for each
resolved issue GM ERP, NMDC.

x. The Management Dashboards has been developed and configured through Qlik
Sense Software in respect of NMDC. The Scope of support comprises
maintenance of existing Dashhoards including addition / deletion / modification
of specific views as per the business requirements.

xi. For changes pertaining to completely new processes, or any new developments
including Dashboards etc., the total efforts (man days) required for the change
requests will be mutually agreed by both the successful bidder and Owner. If the
total efforts required are up to 5 man days, then the successful bidder has to
implement the changes free of cost. In case of changes requiring efforts more
than 5 man days, the successful bidder has to obtain approval of Owner with
financial implications before carrying out the changes. The financial implication
will be calculated by reducing the agreed effort by 5 man days and then
multiplying the balance man days with the rate (per man days) quoted in the
commercial/price bid by the successful bidder. It also includes integration with
third party software planned in future, if any.

xii. The successful bidder has to develop a tracking system Module Wise to record
the issues from “raising of ticket” by the user / ERP Module Core Member till
providing resolution,

xiii. Computers/laptops for office work of staff of successful bidder shall be in the
scope of successful bidder. All other services required to administer, maintain,
update and upgrade the SAP ERP System as per standard of performance
described in the Tender are included in the scope of Services of successful bidder.

xiv. The successful bidder will maintain the SAP ERP System in such manner so as to
minimize operating cost and maximize the system availability.

xv, The successful bidder has to prepare various documents including
Administration Manual, User Manuals, Standard Operating Procedure, Standard
Maintenance Procedure, etc for SAP ERP System and SAP Software.

xvi, The successful bidder shall submit all the necessary documentation (ie,,
Functional Specifications, ‘Technical Specifications, List of affected codes, Test
Scripts, Unit Test Plans, System Integration Test, User Acceptance Test Plans ete.)
for any changes they perform in the system. All these documents should be
approved by NMNC.,

xvii. ‘lhe successful bidder also has to provide all temporary work, ancillary work,
enabling work etc. including provision of tools & software required for
performing Services.

Technical Minimum 8 years of experience in the technical domain having at
Consultants least one (1) Implementation experience as a Technical Lead for
offered ERP product. Development Lead should have expertise in

is essential.
Role of SAP Functional Consultant
a. Assist business users for the problems/issues encountered during day-to-day
business transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System,
pb. Study and evaluate the users’ requirements by interacting with the Core Team
Members & User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an
abstract and algorithmic husiness model or System Requirement Specifications.
c. Realize the solution through standard features of SAP, if standard feature or
alternative solution is not available, solution may be provided through
configuration and customizing the respective business area and ensure to map
the system to achieve the business requirements

d. Provide the Documentation for the settings and Standard Operating
Procedures (SOP) that allow other consultants to do further changes or
modifications if required.

e. Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever
required.

f. Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the
configured scenarios in respect of new developments to be made,

p. Preparation of the documents like As-is-study, Business Blue Print along with
gap analysis and get it approved through Core Team Members before starting
to configuration of future business processes in SAP.

h. Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes
in line with the problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Consultant: ABAP

a. Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

b. Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields,
selection criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code
change

c. Maintenance of Customized Transactions

e. SAP Note applications
f. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP

Role of SAP Technical Consultant: BASIS
a. Review and Analyze Daily, Weekly, Monthly and Annual System Monitoring
Checks.
b. Assignment of Roles and Authorizations to end users as per approval regularly
without delay to ensure no business transactions was halted for want of Roles

i. Support will be extended for Data Migrations for the approved change requests.

ANNEXURE - B
Scope of Work for the proposal of Selection of Service Provider for Annual
Maintenance Contract for SAP S/4 HANA at NMDC
The scope of work in this tender includes
> To provide all the services (onsite support) required for successful and uninterrupted
day to day operations/transactions, maintenance, update and upgrade of the SAP ERP
System at all locations for the following modules of SAP S/4 HANA and entire SAP
landscape implemented in NMDC Limited (NMDC) including developments to be made
as per the requirements of business notified from time to time.

a) FINANCE & CONTROLLING (FICO)

h) MATERIALS MANAGEMENT (MM) INCLUDING SUPPLIER RELATIONSHIP
MANAGEMENT (SRM)

c) PRODUCTION PLANNING (PP)

d) PLANT MAINTENACE (PM)

e) HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT (IICM) / MPLOYEE SELF SERVICE (ESS) /
MANAGEMENT SELIF SERVICE (MSS)

f}) PROJECT SYSTEM (PS)

g) SALES & DISTRIBUTION (SD)

h) QUALITY MANAGEMENT (QM)

i) ENVIRONMENT, HEALTH & SAFETY (EHS)

j) ADVANCED BUSINESS APPLICATION PROGRAMMING (ABAP)

k) BUSINESS APPLICATION SYSTEMS INTEGRATED SOLUTION (BASIS)

1) MANUFACTURING INTEGRATION INTELLIGENCE (MIT)

m) FILE LIFRCYCLE MANAGEMNET (FLM), FIORI APPS, MANAGEMENT
DASHBOARDS, NON-SAP PORTALS (PYTHON BASED PORTAL LIKE MDM, K-
MEASUREMENT BOOK ETC DEVELOPED IN-IIOUSE)

In respect of NMDG, support shall be extended from Head Office, Hyderabad to all
locations. The major locations of NMDC are mentioned hereunder for information:

i. BIOM, Kirandul Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

ii, BIOM, Bacheli Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

iii. DIOM, Donimalai Complex, Bellary (Karnataka)

iv. Pellet Plant, Donimalai, Bellary (Karnataka)

v. DMP, Panna. (Madhya Pradesh)

vi. SIU, Paloncha (Andhra Pradesh) oe

vii. Slurry Pipe Line (SPL), Jagdalpur, (Chhattisgarh)
viii. R&D Centre, Hyderabad (Telangana)
ix. Coal Division, Ranchi
x. Regional Offices Delhi, Raipur, Visakhapatnam, Chennai, Bengaluru, Mumbai,
Bhubaneshwar, Kolkata

However, wherever required, the support team has to travel to respective
locations as per the instructions of NMDC, The Conveyance & Lodging charges
will be borne by owner as per the terms mentioned in this tender.
The successful bidder has to tie up with Existing Contractor for Knowledge
Transfer of the SAP ERP System, software developments, Source / software
codes, drawings, documents, manuals, reports, transactions & maintenance
records ete.
SCOPE OF WORK for SAP Support Service
The broad scope of the Functional and Technical consultant for SAP Support
Services including enhancement / customization of the SAP application in NMDC
mentioned hereunder, It may note that the scope mentioned hereunder is not
exhaustive:
SPECIFIC REQUIREMENT:
i. The successful bidder is required to provide technical & functional support
services which include the identification and rectification of all defects/bugs.

ii, ‘lhe successful bidder is required to assist in providing solution for recovery
from transaction errors, system errors, hardware failures and/or other
application, modification, enhancement issues etc.

iii. The successful bidder is required to assist in performing period end closing
activities (Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually) including Tax reporting,
Regulatory Compliance etc.

iv. ‘The support team should be based at NMDC Head Office, Hyderabad on full time
basis in respect of NMDC to facilitate timely resolution of issues.

v, Enhancements/developments, patches, version upgrades, service packs etc. (as
applicable) are to be installed time to time where ever required on release by
OEM in consultation with the company including third party solutions
implemented and integrated with SAP. Each upgrade should be accompanied by
a detailed document containing the step-by-step procedure for effecting the
changes to the system by ensuing an established change management process.

vi. The successful bidder shall review and adopt the Governance, Risk & Compliance
(GRC) in the system to streamline and have better control across the transactions
of the company.

vii. The Successful Bidder shall assign the Roles and Authorizations to end users as
and when required as per approval without delay to ensure no business
transactions was halted for want of Roles & Authorizations.

viii, The successful bidder shall provide contact numbers, email addresses, escalation
matrix ofa dedicated support team. NMDC shall be keptinformed well in advance
in case any changes are being made in the contact details. .

x


---

Page 646 (pytesseract-ocr):
Ker

ix, ‘The successful bidder shall submit detailed documentation (e.g. Technical
documents, Configuration documents, Functional document if any) for cach
resolved issue GM ERP, NMDC.

x. The Management Dashboards has been developed and configured through Qlik
Sense Software in respect of NMDC. ‘The Scope of support comprises
maintenance of existing Dashboards including addition / deletion / modification
of specific views as per the business requirements.

xi. For changes pertaining to completely new processes, or any new developments
including Dashhoards etc., the total efforts (man days) required for the change
requests will be mutually agreed by both the successful bidder and Owner. If the
total efforts required are up to 5 man days, then the successful bidder has to
implement the changes free of cost, In case of changes requiring efforts more
than 5 man days, the successful bidder has to obtain approval of Owner with
financial implications before carrying out the changes. The financial implication
will be calculated by reducing the agreed effort by 5 man days and then

multiplying the balance man days with the rate (per man days) quoted in the
commercial /price bid by the successful bidder, It also includes integration with
third party software planned in future, ifany.

xii. The successful bidder has to develop a tracking system Module Wise to record
the issues from “raising of ticket" by the user / ERP Module Core Member till
providing resolution.

xiii, Computers/laptops for office work of staff of successful bidder shall be in the
scope of successful bidder. All other services required to administer, maintain,
update and upgrade the SAP ERP System as per standard of performance
described in the Tender are included in the scope of Services of successful bidder.

xiv. The successful bidder will maintain the SAP ERP System in such manner so as to
minimize operating cost and maximize the system availability.

xv. The successful bidder has to prepare various documents including
Administration Manual, User Manuals, Standard Operating Procedure, Standard
Maintenance Procedure, etc for SAP ERP System and SAP Software.

xvi. The successful bidder shall submit all the necessary documentation (ie,
Functional Specifications, Technical Specifications, List of affected codes, Test
Scripts, Unit Test Plans, System Integration Test, User Acceptance Test Plans etc.)
for any changes they perform in the system, All these documents should be
approved by NMDC.

xvii. ‘The successful bidder alsa has to provide all temporary work, ancillary work,
enabling work etc. including provision of tools & software required for
performing Services.

xviii. The successful bidder shall provide adequate training as well as awareness
programs to all users in respective modules or as and when requested by the
Owner without impacting the day to day transactions.

xix, ‘The Basis Consultants of the successful bidder will perform all type of monitoring
and administrative activities like restarting SAP servers, maintenance of backup
data, maintenance of system configuration, administration for Browser-Based
User Dialogs, User Administration (ABAP/JAVA/PYTHON etc.), Creating and
maintaining Remote Function Call (RFC) Connections, Administration and
Maintenance for lixternal Communication, Schedule Background Tasks, Printer
and spool administration / maintenance, Administration and maintenance of
Transport Management System, System Monitoring and ‘l'rouble Shooting,
Setting Up an SAP System Landscape, SLD monitoring, Exporting/importing
Transport Requests, , System Workload Analysis, System trace monitoring,
Performance Analysis, SAP Memory Management , System Load Verification,
Expensive SQL Statements Analysis, SAP Table buffering, Database Overview and
monitoring, DB Monitoring Tools, Recovery, DBA activity through BR*Tools,
Restoring a backup without any additional cost to the Owner.

xx, The successful bidder will perform SAP BASIS activities in Development, Quality
and production SAP S/4 HANA systems. They will also perform all the System
Monitoring and health check activities for smooth running of the servers.

xxi. The successful bidder shall prepare weekly/monthly status reports and submit
to the NMDC.

xxii. The successful bidder shall take up any SAP product level issues with SAP
through respective module lead of NMDC, and shall also follow up on the same
for quick resolution.

xxiii, IF any support is required pertaining to SAP applications in DC & DR siles then
same shall be extended by the successful bidder to the DATA CENTER (DC) &
DISASTER RECOVERY (DR) site without any additional cost.

xxiv, The successful bidder will follow the holiday calendar of NMDC. They have to
work late after working hours and attend issues on weekends/holidays as and
when required without any additional cost to NMDC.

xxv. Successful bidder shall keep each & every record, logs of the proceedings of cach
phases of the project in writing to avoid any debatable issues of delaying the
project on accounts of reasons attributable to both the sides

xxvi. Successful bidder shall submit monthly detailed report to GM ERP NMDC on
Helpdesk ticket analysis with all status, Change Request status and status of all
service requests logged with OEM SAP and also make suitable presentation to
the management on the above issues as and when required. Additionally,
successful bidder may share their comments on quality of tickets, any major

all these equipments, systems, and structures of the SAP ERP System as per the

NMDC.

xxviii. The scope of Services shall also include all such services which may nol have

Operating and Maintenance Practices and such services shall be performed by
the successful bidder without any additional cost to the NMDC.

The above resource requirement is exclusively for support services, in respect of

NMDC management may take interview at the time of deployment of the resources.
Any change in staff will be done based on mutually agreement.
If any of the deployed resource is unable to attend for work for any day, the
successful hidder has to provide suitable resource immediately with the approval
of GM ERP NMDC. If any resource is absent for more than 2 days and not been
provided additional equivalent resource as stated above, an amount equal Lo per
man-day cost quoted at price schedule will be recovered / deducted from the
monthly bill payable to the successful bidder. However, if Owner feels that such
absenteeism is impacting the progress of work, Owner reserves the right to take
action like termination of contract under default of the contractor.
The successful bidder will follow the holiday calendar of NMDC. They should deploy
the resources during all the working days from Monday to Saturday (9:30 AM to
6:00 PM). The successful bidder team has to work late after working hours online /
offline to address the issues of all Locations and on weekends/holidays as and when
required without any additional cost.
Minimum qualifications requirement for resources:
Project Manager | BE / B. TECH / MBA / MCA/ CA/ ICWA degree with minimum 10
(Lead years of total experience with 7 years relevant experience of SAP
Consultant) Implementation, He should have successfully completed at least
three (3) SAP implementation projects out of which he should
have worked as Project Manager in at least two (2) projects. He
should have knowledge of cross-functional processes.
Functional BE / B. TECH / MBA / MCA / CA/ ICWA degree with minimum 5
Consultants years of total experience with 3 years relevant experience of SAP
Implementation. He should have successfully completed at least
two (2) SAP implementation projects out of which he should have
worked as Lead Consultant in at least one (1) projects.

Technical | Minimum 8 years of experience in the technical domain having at

Consultants least one (1) Implementation experience as a Technical Lead for
offered ERP product. Development lead should have expertise in
development work. System administration lead should have
expertise in system administration work. The personnel should
have experience in one of the following Technical area (Master
Data & Governance, Workflow, System Administrators,
Developers, Developers (Interface), Management Reporting).
Certification in the ERP function of the respective technical Irad
is essential.

Role of SAP Functional Consultant

a. Assist business users for the problems/issucs cncountered during day-to-day
business transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System.

b. Study and evaluate the users’ requirements by interacting with the Core Team
Members & User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an
abstract and algorithmic business model or System Requirement Specifications.

c. Realize the solution through standard features of SAP, if standard feature or
alternative solution is not available, solution may be provided through
configuration and customizing the respective business area and ensure to map
the system to achieve the business requirements

d. Provide the Nocumentation for the settings and Standard Operating
Procedures (SOP) that allow other consultants to do further changes or
modifications if required.

e, Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever
required,

f. Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the
configured scenarios in respect of new developments to be made.

py. Preparation of the documents like As-is-study, Business Blue Print along with
gap analysis and get it approved through Core Team Members before starting
to configuration of future business processes in SAP.

h. Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes
in line with the problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Consultant; ABAP

a. Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

b. Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields,
sclection criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code
change

c, Maintenance of Customized Transactions

Noe"
d. Performance tuning
e. SAP Note applications
f. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP
g. Working closely with functional teams
h. Support in analyzing & resolving the dump issues reported by the users
Role of SAP Technical Consultant: BASIS
a. Review and Analyze Daily, Weekly, Monthly and Annual System Monitoring
Checks.
b. Assignment of Roles and Authorizations to end users as per approval regularly
| without delay to ensure no business transactions was halted for want of Roles
& Authorizations. (Like: CIS move, User & Profile assignment and small
correction in profile)
c. Troubleshoot and rectify the problems on day to day hasis
d. Client copy / system refresh (whenever required at least once ina year)
e. SAP note applications with reference to SAP Basis
f. SNC configuration (whenever required at least once ina year).
g. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP Basis
h. Performance Tuning on Periodic Basis and ensure 99,9% uptime
j. Support will be extended for Data Migrations for the approved change requests.
‘The upload data formats will be provided by the Consultants. NMDC will provide
the cleansed and validated data to the Support team.

650
ANNEXURE - B
Scope of Work for the proposal of Selection of Service Provider for Annual
Maintenance Contract for SAP S/4 HANA at NMDC
The scope of work in this tender includes
> To provide all the services (onsite support) required for successful and uninterrupted
day to day operations/transactions, maintenance, update and upgrade of the SAP ERP
System at all locations for the following modules of SAP S/4 HANA and entire SAP
landscape implemented in NMDC Limited (NMDC) including developments to be made
as per the requirements of business notified from time to time.

a) FINANCE & CONTROLLING (FICO)

h) MATERIALS MANAGEMENT (MM) INCLUDING SUPPLIER RELATIONSHIP
MANAGEMENT (SRM)

c) PRODUCTION PLANNING (PP)

d) PLANT MAINTENACE (PM)

e) HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT (IICM) / MPLOYEE SELF SERVICE (ESS) /
MANAGEMENT SELIF SERVICE (MSS)

f}) PROJECT SYSTEM (PS)

g) SALES & DISTRIBUTION (SD)

h) QUALITY MANAGEMENT (QM)

i) ENVIRONMENT, HEALTH & SAFETY (EHS)

j) ADVANCED BUSINESS APPLICATION PROGRAMMING (ABAP)

k) BUSINESS APPLICATION SYSTEMS INTEGRATED SOLUTION (BASIS)

1) MANUFACTURING INTEGRATION INTELLIGENCE (MIT)

m) FILE LIFRCYCLE MANAGEMNET (FLM), FIORI APPS, MANAGEMENT
DASHBOARDS, NON-SAP PORTALS (PYTHON BASED PORTAL LIKE MDM, K-
MEASUREMENT BOOK ETC DEVELOPED IN-IIOUSE)

In respect of NMDG, support shall be extended from Head Office, Hyderabad to all
locations. The major locations of NMDC are mentioned hereunder for information:

i. BIOM, Kirandul Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

ii, BIOM, Bacheli Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

iii. DIOM, Donimalai Complex, Bellary (Karnataka)

iv. Pellet Plant, Donimalai, Bellary (Karnataka)

v. DMP, Panna. (Madhya Pradesh)

vi. SIU, Paloncha (Andhra Pradesh) oe

vii. Slurry Pipe Line (SPL), Jagdalpur, (Chhattisgarh)
viii. R&D Centre, Hyderabad (Telangana)
ix. Coal Division, Ranchi
x. Regional Offices Delhi, Raipur, Visakhapatnam, Chennai, Bengaluru, Mumbai,
Bhubaneshwar, Kolkata

Page 660 (pytesseract-ocr):
However, wherever required, the support team has to travel to respective
locations as per the instructions of NMDC, The Conveyance & Lodging charges
will be borne by owner as per the terms mentioned in this tender.
The successful bidder has to tie up with Existing Contractor for Knowledge
Transfer of the SAP ERP System, software developments, Source / software
codes, drawings, documents, manuals, reports, transactions & maintenance
records ete.
SCOPE OF WORK for SAP Support Service
The broad scope of the Functional and Technical consultant for SAP Support
Services including enhancement / customization of the SAP application in NMDC
mentioned hereunder, It may note that the scope mentioned hereunder is not
exhaustive:
SPECIFIC REQUIREMENT:
i. The successful bidder is required to provide technical & functional support
services which include the identification and rectification of all defects/bugs.

ii, ‘lhe successful bidder is required to assist in providing solution for recovery
from transaction errors, system errors, hardware failures and/or other
application, modification, enhancement issues etc.

iii. The successful bidder is required to assist in performing period end closing
activities (Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually) including Tax reporting,
Regulatory Compliance etc.

iv. ‘The support team should be based at NMDC Head Office, Hyderabad on full time
basis in respect of NMDC to facilitate timely resolution of issues.

v, Enhancements/developments, patches, version upgrades, service packs etc. (as
applicable) are to be installed time to time where ever required on release by
OEM in consultation with the company including third party solutions
implemented and integrated with SAP. Each upgrade should be accompanied by
a detailed document containing the step-by-step procedure for effecting the
changes to the system by ensuing an established change management process.

vi. The successful bidder shall review and adopt the Governance, Risk & Compliance
(GRC) in the system to streamline and have better control across the transactions
of the company.

Role of SAP Functional Consultant

a. Assist business users for the problems/issucs cncountered during day-to-day
business transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System.

b. Study and evaluate the users’ requirements by interacting with the Core Team
Members & User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an
abstract and algorithmic business model or System Requirement Specifications.

c. Realize the solution through standard features of SAP, if standard feature or
alternative solution is not available, solution may be provided through
configuration and customizing the respective business area and ensure to map
the system to achieve the business requirements

d. Provide the Nocumentation for the settings and Standard Operating
Procedures (SOP) that allow other consultants to do further changes or
modifications if required.

e, Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever
required,

f. Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the
configured scenarios in respect of new developments to be made.

py. Preparation of the documents like As-is-study, Business Blue Print along with
gap analysis and get it approved through Core Team Members before starting
to configuration of future business processes in SAP.

h. Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes
in line with the problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Consultant; ABAP

a. Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

b. Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields,
sclection criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code
change

c, Maintenance of Customized Transactions
